features.evidence: 1 storage but yeah it s been butter the whole time i am very happy to report now it s still a pixel so i have to add the disclaimer that it might not stay this way pixels have a history of this accelerated aging which is even more of a bummer when you pay this much for a phone but i can t report on that until we get to a year from now so the best i can do is tell you how it s acting now and it is acting great the other thing though that i m hoping changes over time is the battery life because the battery life in the first this week that i ve been testing the phone has not been great usually i can just throw away the first day or two of me testing this one because it s still setting it up still connecting everything and getting it working properly and then i can start looking at the numbers but every day since i first got this phone i ve had the same experience which is not that great four hours of screen on time at the end of my day which is not nearly as high as some of the flagships out there it is a 4 800 milliamp hour battery which is really good for a foldable but not gigantic for a giant screen on the inside so pixels have this feature called adaptive battery and sometimes they can get a little bit better over time depends on your use depends on who you are your mileage may vary but hey i ve gotten i would say this is c plus battery life and the more i think about it i ve been using a lot of the phone wall closed which means it s the smaller outside screen and so if i had been using the larger inside screen even more it would not have gone better so yeah c c plus for battery but i want to talk about the software experience here because it is a pixel after all and there is one weird thing so this was a very interesting phone to test because google had to do a lot of software work and i was excited to try all of it google s apps are all these beautiful tablet optimized apps now they re so thoughtfully designed for the phone with this size screen they all look normal on the outside screen but on the inside google drive has this nice slide out sidebar and multi column view gmail s new multi column view is great the weather app the new weather app is absolutely fantastic google calendar is completely redesigned there s just so much good stuff and then the new dock ties it all together so this dock i talked about on the pixel tablet you swipe up slightly from the bottom and the home bar turns into your home screen dock so this is the the apps that you have on the bottom of your home screen and you can quickly switch between these apps or launch into multitasking super easily so just the other folding phones i feel i feel i m multitasking god when i m using this huge folding phone open and just just type it away but here is the weird part as far as i can remember every other foldable phone allows you to set up the home screens and stuff on the outside screen and then the inside screen is separate you can have a separate set of widgets you can go crazy and make a different layout on the inside here and on this phone you cannot do that so look right now my outside screen is these three pages this one with the icons in the weather and then this calendar widget one and then this third one so when i open up the phone that s exactly mirrored here the left one is the first home screen then the right one is the second one and i swipe over and here s the third one but if i take this raindrop icon and move it over here and then when i go back to closed it s also moved the raindrop icon there because it s keeping them exactly mirrored at all times which is fine i think that makes a lot of sense especially for beginners i i bet if if apple made a folding iphone right now they would do the exact same thing because iphone users are used to not having a ton of customization on their home screens but i haven t found a way to unlock that at all there s no i haven t seen any home screen setting or display setting to be able to set up a different home screen on the inside versus the outside just feels on android to me i feel i can t be the only one who s spending eighteen hundred dollars on a first gen folding phone who would to have some apps on the outside screen and then a bunch of widgets on the inside screen you can t even span the middle with one widget because it s treating them as two separate home screens even though it s all with one big folding screen i just found that so weird so anyway aside from that i really enjoyed using the pixel fold seriously between the great performance and the google software all the pixel stuff google assistant call screening etc it s really easy to get past the weirdness the other thing you typically expect out of a pixel though is good cameras so let s talk about this camera situation for a little bit because as i ve said everywhere you look there s just a little bit of weird so first of all there are five cameras on this phone there is the nine megapixel hole punch camera on the front on the outside screen then there s an eight megapixel camera on the inside up in that bezel then there s the three primary cameras on the back the thing with these back cameras is because of how thin the phone is there s definitely not enough room to physically fit the same size sensors and optics that you have in the flagship pixel phone so there is all new stuff here and it s a little bit smaller so there s a 48 megapixel main camera a 10 megapixel ultra wide and a 10 megapixel 5x telephoto pixel fold photos feel a lot pixel 7a photos which isn t an insult that s just to say that they re in the same style the same contrasty pixel style but overall are definitely a step down in quality from the flagships they re a bit noisier a bit more muted in the colors especially with the ultra wide and it takes a bit longer to gather the same amount of light as the larger flagship sensors also if you want to see video from the pixel fold i shot the entire last autofocus video on the pixel fold so you can clearly see it s a step down from some of the other flagships i ve shot with there s a lot of noise and the darker areas of the frame but i will say it was definitely easier to shoot with because it s a folding phone so i can just unfold it and i see the viewfinder and i can frame things up with the primary camera so that s an advantage but yeah it s not you re gonna go out and shoot movies on this thing samsung has had to deal with the same type of thing the the camera quality of their super expensive foldable phones has always been a slight step down from their big flagship slab style phones so i think that s a fair comparison this is a step it s a notch down from the pixel flagship cameras what s fun this is a this is technically a first gen device right this is the first time google s ever made a foldable and on one hand you can tell that they ve at least learned some things from the other foldables already out there because this feels a little bit better in some ways they ve gone with this passport sized form factor and they ve made conscious decisions to make it thin and there s some hinge innovation here and it s kept the pixel aesthetic just a lot of good choices that are impressive for a first gen device but it s still clearly has some first gen shortcomings also that have me thinking well maybe the next one will be better for the inside display the crease and the bezels and the 178 degree almost flat fold maybe the next one will be better for the weak battery life yes there is wireless charging but hey maybe the next one will have faster charging than the current max of 30 watts and of course the app situation is still let s just say it s clearly imperfect so they ve done a lot of good stuff with their software but some apps still look horrible look at instagram it just it looks terrible you can double tap to the left or to the right to move it over closer to the side where it s more reachable but still that that is rough twitter also looks horrible my golf app 18 birdies also not optimized even youtube studio is just not optimized for this bigger screen and the list goes on and on and on maybe maybe that ll be better by the next one so then the last weird thing about this phone is it is one thousand eight hundred dollars so of course that that prices out pretty much anyone who s just gonna walk into a store casually and just get a normal upgrade for their phone it will be relegated to the early adopters the enthusiasts maybe people watching this video and the pixel fans it s a smaller group but also i gotta say weirdly enough i really using this phone i love using this phone it s got just the right set of quirks and features to that that i it over the regular pixel which i had been using and also i think it s it s specifically because it s so well optimized for using it closed i keep talking about how it s the best foldable to use closed it s got the right set of strengths and weaknesses that i spend most of my time just enjoying using it a normal phone close and then every once in a while my 20 30 40 of the time that i open it up and use it it s just this whole new world and it s a nice benefit it s getting closer to that ideal folding phone for me i really it it s also android 14 coming up soon five years of promise security updates great haptics great speakers this there s a lot of it s got just enough upside that i really it i m sure i m looking forward to the next one still of course but for anyone else who s going to jump in on this first gen version just know what you re getting into it s a weird one but i it also you might have spotted a few designs from channel sponsor dbrand a few times in this video this new one is called robot city and i think it s pretty sick it s one of my favorite designs they ve released yet it helps a lot also on a phone that says monotonous as the pixel fold so these designs have become a staple of dbrand over the years i don t know if you remember the the hair dryer that s been in the studio which was wrapped in the original robot skin so this new one robot city if you can look closely you ll see it there are three different versions that you can choose from there were all these unique discreet pieces of art first one is called dead quarters a dystopian headquarters of robot city the second one is amusement park complete with sharks and krakens and doomed carnival rides and the third one really hits home for me it s called crime square because times square but with crime there are so many easter eggs in them it s silly so if you want to check them out the robot city skins are available on over 100 devices including the pixel folds if you want to check them out i ll have a link down below in the description either way that s been it till the next one thanks for watching catch you later peace foreign However, and this isn t a silicon carbon battery or anything that charges particularly fast either

6 inch folding amoled display also 120 hertz and also plenty bright up to 1450 nits really nice size it s reminiscent of that oppo find n2 foldable but there are a couple of things to note here s where some of the weird starts so first of all the crease is pretty average it s definitely not dramatically worse than others i ve seen but you can for sure see it if you re looking for it and you can feel it but because it s through the middle of the screen you aren t usually running your finger over it unlike the vertically folding phones so it s not really a big deal it s easy to disappear to your eyes as you just look at content and use it normally but then there was just some talk about how it didn t seem the phone fully unfolds to 180 degrees and well i don t know this hinge is really firm so you can fold it up halfway or any angle you want really easily which is awesome but then when you fold it flat visually it looks pretty flat but when you put it on a table since there s a camera bump it becomes more clear as it s rocking back and forth it s maybe one or two degrees short of flat it s weird you can you can force it to get to flat or even a little bit past that but it really doesn t feel good to do that anyway then there is the bezel as you ve probably seen by now this phone does have a slightly bigger top and bottom bezel than some other folding phones it is noticeable at first when you just look at the bezels but again it s one of those things where you start using the phone and it is easy to forget about i think a lot of videos are going to hold it up side by side next to the samsung fold and then it s oh yeah clearly one of them has a dramatically thinner bezel and it is really noticeable but just using a phone it looks fine and google says that this was an intentional decision so what they were saying is they were again biasing so much towards it being thin that for some of the hinge components they move them out and above and below the hinge instead of in that z axis space so that it could be as thin as it is but now you end up with a little bit of extra bezel above oven below the screen but it did allow them room to put in a real webcam or selfie camera instead of a under display one but also i think this is a little bit along the lines of google the pixel has never really been all about pristine super highly engineered amazing hardware they ve flexed their strengths on the software side and i think there s a little bit of that here too either way you can check the box of ipx8 water resistance which is nice i do really this satin finish on the back which does a good job of minimizing fingerprints and looks pretty good and the camera bump doesn t go all the way to the very edges to meet the rails of the phone that would have been tough with the foldable but i do still think they did a good job of maintaining the whole pixel aesthetic performance is weirdly good it s it s really really good it s the best of any pixel phone even though it s using the same chip it s the tensor g2 from the pixel 7 and 7 pro but it s yeah it feels smoother and faster and more consistent than the pixel 7 and 7 pro so maybe it could be that 12 gigs of ram that are in here it also has 256 or 512 gigs of ufs 3 However, it still has a selfie camera cut out in the corner

hey what s up i m kim hd here yo the pixel fold is a weird phone there are so many things about this thing that are unusual so i and many other people have been waiting for a folding pixel this is something we imagined was coming eventually so it is surreal to be holding and have been using and testing this phone i ve been using it for about a week now and boy do i have a lot of thoughts so i guess let s just start with the design i really a lot of the decisions they ve made with this foldable which really optimize far towards it being thin and passport shaped originally when i first got to play with it i was saying man this could be the best foldable to use wall closed and i do now stand by that take it s the easiest best foldable to use when it s closed but unfortunately it does have a much lower ranking for using it while open so the front screen is 5

8 inches diagonally it s got these curved edges you can see and it s fairly thin bezels all the way around the rails all the way around the phone itself and the hinge are stainless steel and the whole thing is relatively thin it s just bordering on a little bit thicker than the thickest normal phone you can get i think the number is 12 millimeters total you can see there s no air gap in the middle so honestly yeah i ve spent a lot of time using the outside screen a normal phone and it s just the most reachable usable outside screen of any folding phone you unlock it with the fingerprint reader on the side which is also the power button and it s pretty quick this is a 120 hertz oled display that gets up to 1550 nits peak brightness so it s plenty visible outside it looks really good to me in most situations i m impressed this is a premium outside display which i guess i should be for eighteen hundred dollars but we ll we ll get to the price a little bit later the point is though i i ve always said the best foldables have to be usable as just a regular phone too and this one does accomplish that great so then you open up the phone and then that s when things start to get a little more weird so you can t exactly open this phone with one hand i ve definitely tried i try this with every foldable some are easier than others this one s pretty much a no go but once you get it open when you re in there you re greeted with an interior 7 However, and even in daily performance, there are times when the phone stutters, which is not good for a phone that costs upwards of 1

really doesn t feel good to do that anyway then there is the bezel as you ve probably seen by now this phone does have a slightly bigger top and bottom bezel than some other folding phones it is noticeable at first when you just look at the bezels but again it s one of those things where you start using the phone and it is easy to forget about i think a lot of videos are going to hold it up side by side next to the samsung fold and then it s oh yeah clearly one of them has a dramatically thinner bezel and it is really noticeable but just using a phone it looks fine and google says that this was an intentional decision so what they were saying is they were again biasing so much towards it being thin that for some of the hinge components they move them out and above and below the hinge instead of in that z axis space so that it could be as thin as it is but now you end up with a little bit of extra bezel above oven below the screen but it did allow them room to put in a real webcam or selfie camera instead of a under display one but also i think this is a little bit along the lines of google the pixel has never really been all about pristine super highly engineered amazing hardware they ve flexed their strengths on the software side and i think there s a little bit of that here too either way you can check the box of ipx8 water resistance which is nice i do really this satin finish on the back which does a good job of minimizing fingerprints and looks pretty good and the camera bump doesn t go all the way to the very edges to meet the rails of the phone that would have been tough with the foldable but i do still think they did a good job of maintaining the whole pixel aesthetic performance is weirdly good it s it s really really good it s the best of any pixel phone even though it s using the same chip it s the tensor g2 from the pixel 7 and 7 pro but it s yeah it feels smoother and faster and more consistent than the pixel 7 and 7 pro so maybe it could be that 12 gigs of ram that are in here it also has 256 or 512 gigs of ufs 3

5 megapixel selfie camera which is just google doing maths on the selfie camera but again very consistent selfies natural looking photos looks really good but the number one thing to love about any google phone is the software google devices are never known for having great hardware but they make it up for the absolutely best software so is it the same true for the pixel fold well if you remember we did a video on pixel tablet and google apps work really good on the pixel tablet compared to any other android tablet out in the market same is the case with this pixel phone here i ll open gmail on galaxy fold 5 and then i ll open here on pixel fold gmail is better optimized for the bigger display same i ll do it with google play store see on pixel fold it is optimized for the larger display now this optimization doesn t happen with social media apps or third party apps twitter instagram they work the same on both the folding phones but google apps especially are optimized for this bigger wider display and whenever i open an app in full screen i just have to swipe up from the bottom you get the dock and from here i can drag and drop any app to go to split screen those animations and in general the entire software is smooth the inner display exactly behaves the pixel tablet or a tablet up until now these three things make the pixel 4 the best folding phone out there right but here s where things started going downward first of all the pixel fold comes with google tensor g2 12gb ram 250 cb storage and i don t know enough videos don t point this out google pixel heats we have the google pixel 7a with the same tensor g2 chip it hits simply even if you download a netflix episode on 5g and we have so many comments from our team tw community about pixels heating issues same is the case with a pixel fold in india weather it heats quite a lot we did some benchmark we did a cpu throttling test which runs your cpu at 100 for 15 minutes and the graph turns out yellowish red and i even played bjmi during normal use on it and after a game you see the fps starts dropping google has to do something about the tensor chip in indian weather you feel the heat battery lane everything and i m not saying using a one year old processor is bad it s fine even if it is not the latest and greatest snapdragon 8 gen 2 it s fine but tensor g2 has its own issues and even if you ignore the tensor g2 there s this hinge for some reason the phone shuts completely there is literally no gap but when you open it slightly bend and you can now apply pressure to make it completely flat but you have to do this every time when you open the phone and hinge is very important part when it comes to the durability of the folding foot and in order to make the phone shut completely flat google had to bring out all the hardware folding parts to the front and this is how the inner bezel looks if i place the z fold 5 beside it just by looking at the bezel you will feel the pixel fold too is two generations and this this feature is one of the biggest thing see i use the z45 in two ways this here on the cover screen is my work productivity mode so i have all the banking apps my to do list slack whatsapp everything on the cover screen and when i have to just chill or go back home i unfold the phone and i go to the inner screen and then i have bgmi here netflix disney plus hot star to watch india versus pakistan match washed out in greens sad but v1 v1 at the end so you get the point it makes me feel this folding phone has two different phones in it this is the work mode and this is my normal when i open it s entertainment tablet mode that samsung in one ui understands here on the pixel see if i just drag and drop whatsapp here at the bottom on the cover screen and now i unfold it i also have whatsapp on the inner screen so it reads both the outer and inner home screen as one now the pixel phone costs about 17

review_summary: 1 storage but yeah it s been butter the whole time i am very happy to report now it s still a pixel so i have to add the disclaimer that it might not stay this way pixels have a history of this accelerated aging which is even more of a bummer when you pay this much for a phone but i can t report on that until we get to a year from now so the best i can do is tell you how it s acting now and it is acting great the other thing though that i m hoping changes over time is the battery life because the battery life in the first this week that i ve been testing the phone has not been great usually i can just throw away the first day or two of me testing this one because it s still setting it up still connecting everything and getting it working properly and then i can start looking at the numbers but every day since i first got this phone i ve had the same experience which is not that great four hours of screen on time at the end of my day which is not nearly as high as some of the flagships out there it is a 4 800 milliamp hour battery which is really good for a foldable but not gigantic for a giant screen on the inside so pixels have this feature called adaptive battery and sometimes they can get a little bit better over time depends on your use depends on who you are your mileage may vary but hey i ve gotten i would say this is c plus battery life and the more i think about it i ve been using a lot of the phone wall closed which means it s the smaller outside screen and so if i had been using the larger inside screen even more it would not have gone better so yeah c c plus for battery but i want to talk about the software experience here because it is a pixel after all and there is one weird thing so this was a very interesting phone to test because google had to do a lot of software work and i was excited to try all of it google s apps are all these beautiful tablet optimized apps now they re so thoughtfully designed for the phone with this size screen they all look normal on the outside screen but on the inside google drive has this nice slide out sidebar and multi column view gmail s new multi column view is great the weather app the new weather app is absolutely fantastic google calendar is completely redesigned there s just so much good stuff and then the new dock ties it all together so this dock i talked about on the pixel tablet you swipe up slightly from the bottom and the home bar turns into your home screen dock so this is the the apps that you have on the bottom of your home screen and you can quickly switch between these apps or launch into multitasking super easily so just the other folding phones i feel i feel i m multitasking god when i m using this huge folding phone open and just just type it away but here is the weird part as far as i can remember every other foldable phone allows you to set up the home screens and stuff on the outside screen and then the inside screen is separate you can have a separate set of widgets you can go crazy and make a different layout on the inside here and on this phone you cannot do that so look right now my outside screen is these three pages this one with the icons in the weather and then this calendar widget one and then this third one so when i open up the phone that s exactly mirrored here the left one is the first home screen then the right one is the second one and i swipe over and here s the third one but if i take this raindrop icon and move it over here and then when i go back to closed it s also moved the raindrop icon there because it s keeping them exactly mirrored at all times which is fine i think that makes a lot of sense especially for beginners i i bet if if apple made a folding iphone right now they would do the exact same thing because iphone users are used to not having a ton of customization on their home screens but i haven t found a way to unlock that at all there s no i haven t seen any home screen setting or display setting to be able to set up a different home screen on the inside versus the outside just feels on android to me i feel i can t be the only one who s spending eighteen hundred dollars on a first gen folding phone who would to have some apps on the outside screen and then a bunch of widgets on the inside screen you can t even span the middle with one widget because it s treating them as two separate home screens even though it s all with one big folding screen i just found that so weird so anyway aside from that i really enjoyed using the pixel fold seriously between the great performance and the google software all the pixel stuff google assistant call screening etc it s really easy to get past the weirdness the other thing you typically expect out of a pixel though is good cameras so let s talk about this camera situation for a little bit because as i ve said everywhere you look there s just a little bit of weird so first of all there are five cameras on this phone there is the nine megapixel hole punch camera on the front on the outside screen then there s an eight megapixel camera on the inside up in that bezel then there s the three primary cameras on the back the thing with these back cameras is because of how thin the phone is there s definitely not enough room to physically fit the same size sensors and optics that you have in the flagship pixel phone so there is all new stuff here and it s a little bit smaller so there s a 48 megapixel main camera a 10 megapixel ultra wide and a 10 megapixel 5x telephoto pixel fold photos feel a lot pixel 7a photos which isn t an insult that s just to say that they re in the same style the same contrasty pixel style but overall are definitely a step down in quality from the flagships they re a bit noisier a bit more muted in the colors especially with the ultra wide and it takes a bit longer to gather the same amount of light as the larger flagship sensors also if you want to see video from the pixel fold i shot the entire last autofocus video on the pixel fold so you can clearly see it s a step down from some of the other flagships i ve shot with there s a lot of noise and the darker areas of the frame but i will say it was definitely easier to shoot with because it s a folding phone so i can just unfold it and i see the viewfinder and i can frame things up with the primary camera so that s an advantage but yeah it s not you re gonna go out and shoot movies on this thing samsung has had to deal with the same type of thing the the camera quality of their super expensive foldable phones has always been a slight step down from their big flagship slab style phones so i think that s a fair comparison this is a step it s a notch down from the pixel flagship cameras what s fun this is a this is technically a first gen device right this is the first time google s ever made a foldable and on one hand you can tell that they ve at least learned some things from the other foldables already out there because this feels a little bit better in some ways they ve gone with this passport sized form factor and they ve made conscious decisions to make it thin and there s some hinge innovation here and it s kept the pixel aesthetic just a lot of good choices that are impressive for a first gen device but it s still clearly has some first gen shortcomings also that have me thinking well maybe the next one will be better for the inside display the crease and the bezels and the 178 degree almost flat fold maybe the next one will be better for the weak battery life yes there is wireless charging but hey maybe the next one will have faster charging than the current max of 30 watts and of course the app situation is still let s just say it s clearly imperfect so they ve done a lot of good stuff with their software but some apps still look horrible look at instagram it just it looks terrible you can double tap to the left or to the right to move it over closer to the side where it s more reachable but still that that is rough twitter also looks horrible my golf app 18 birdies also not optimized even youtube studio is just not optimized for this bigger screen and the list goes on and on and on maybe maybe that ll be better by the next one so then the last weird thing about this phone is it is one thousand eight hundred dollars so of course that that prices out pretty much anyone
